_comment: Grandfathered timezone-audit findings. `--check` fails only on findings NOT listed here, so the known backlog cannot grow. Keyed by file + rule + matched source text (line numbers churn). Regenerate with `audit_timezone_usage.py --refresh-baseline` — and only ever shrink it.

findings.rule: tz-blind day window — use `local_day_bounds(date, tz)` with .gte/.lt
findings.snippet: db.client.table("workouts").select( "id, name, estimated_calories, completed_at, generation_source, generation_method" ).eq("user_id", user_id).eq("is_completed", True).gte( "completed_at", f"{today}T
This used to be a bare f-string (`f"{start_date}T00:00:00"`), which meant
return f"{raw}T23:59:59" if end_of_day else f"{raw}T00:00:00"

findings.snippet: supabase.client.table("users") \ .select("id, email, name, timezone, created_at") \ .gte("created_at", f"{window_start}T00:00:00")

findings.snippet: supabase.client.table("users") \ .select("id, email, name, timezone, created_at") \ .gte("created_at", f"{window_start}T00:00:00") \ .lt("created_at", f"{window_end}T23:59:59")

closed interval on a half-open bound — `local_day_bounds`/`local_range_bounds` ends need `.lt`, not `.lte`
findings.snippet: sb.client.table("workouts").select( "id, name, exercises_json, completed_at, duration_minutes, " "is_completed, scheduled_date" ).eq("user_id", user_id).gte( "scheduled_date", start_iso ).lte("schedul

findings.rule: closed interval on a half-open bound — `local_day_bounds`/`local_range_bounds` ends need `.lt`, not `.lte`
findings.snippet: sb.client.table("workouts").select( "id, name, template_id, exercises_json, scheduled_date" ).eq("user_id", user_id).gte( "scheduled_date", start_iso ).lte("scheduled_date", end_iso)
end_of_day = sched + "T23:59:59.999999+00:00" if len(sched) == 10 else sched
``user_today_str`` is required — never fall back to ``date.today()``
end_of_day = sched + "T23:59:59.999999+00:00" if len(sched) == 10 else sched
f"{date}T00:00:00" bounds that Postgres resolved at the session zone
effective_to = to_date + "T23:59:59.999999+00:00" if len(to_date) == 10 else to_date
effective_from = from_date + "T00:00:00+00:00" if len(from_date) == 10 else from_date

findings.snippet: Bare UTC `date.today()` would mis-day workouts for any user west of
midnight — pass the timezone-resolved date, never ``date.today()``
